title: Pico Project Swan Leak
post_text:
A flagship XR headset weighing just 270g with 4,000 PPI microOLED panels is coming.

Leaked SDK videos reveal Pico's 'Project Swan,' a high-end device designed to compete with the Apple Vision Pro and Samsung Galaxy XR.

According to Road to VR, the design shifts to a separate battery unit and a woven headstrap to reduce head-worn weight.

Performance is the core focus. Pico claims a co-processor architecture combining custom silicon and a flagship SoC will deliver over 2x the CPU and GPU performance of the XR2 Gen 2.

With an average of 40 pixels per degree (PPD) and a center sweet spot over 45 PPD, the visual fidelity is targeting the professional and high-end consumer market.

While the global launch is set for late 2026, the industry is watching to see if this will be the first device to integrate Qualcomm's next-gen Snapdragon XR chipset.

How will the shift toward external battery packs impact the adoption of standalone XR headsets in professional training environments?

#VirtualReality #XR #Pico #HardwareDesign #SpatialComputing
headline: PICO PROJECT SWAN LEAKED
details: Leaked tutorial videos from Pico's public SDK reveal 'Project Swan,' the company's next flagship XR headset.
The design features a separate battery unit and a woven headstrap, mirroring the form factor of the Apple Vision Pro and Samsung Galaxy XR.
Hardware specifications include microOLED panels with 4,000 pixels per inch (PPI) and an average of 40 pixels per degree (PPD), with a center sweet spot exceeding 45 PPD.
The device is expected to weigh 270g (excluding the battery) and will feature a co-processor combining custom XR silicon with a flagship SoC.
Pico claims this architecture will deliver more than 2x the CPU and GPU performance of the XR2 Gen 2 chipset.
The headset is slated for a global launch in late 2026, though official pricing and full specifications remain unannounced.
